chunks.text: My mother began studying English at sixty.
Having been a housewife for long years
she made the decision when her grandchild was born.
At first, she struggled even with pronunciation.
Trying to make sounds her mouth was not used to
she would stretch her lips again and again.
She patiently corrected herself from reading tap as " tapu.
But my mother was willing.
She would get up at six every morning and listen to radio English conversation.
After breakfast, she would read the textbook for an hour
rubbing the words into her memory.
After half a year, she could handle simple daily conversation.
After a year, she gave directions to a tourist.
After three years, she wrote an English essay at a university extension course.
My mother's progress was somewhat slow, but steady.
The secret was " keep doing it every day.
" Even quite short time, continued for 365 days, becomes a vast sum of hours.
When her grandchild turned five, she began speaking English with him.
The grandchild was amused to see his grandmother speak English.
Then, he too began learning English.
My mother's story teaches me something important.
" Too late " is only an excuse.
If the desire to learn is real, one can begin at any age.
Modern society imposes the pressure on young people that " you must work hard while young.
" But in an age where people live to a hundred
learning is a lifelong pursuit.
My mother is sixty - five this year.
Soon she plans her first solo trip abroad.
The destination is London.
To walk the world on her own feet in English.
That is her new dream.
